# Proverbs 19

## Verses (29)

| # | Basic English Bible | Compare |
|---|---|---|
| [1](BBE.md#v1) | Better is the poor man whose ways are upright, than the man of wealth whose ways are twisted. | [WEB](WEB.md#v1) · [KJV](KJV.md#v1) · [ASV](ASV.md#v1) |
| [2](BBE.md#v2) | Further, without knowledge desire is not good; and he who is over-quick in acting goes out of the right way. | [WEB](WEB.md#v2) · [KJV](KJV.md#v2) · [ASV](ASV.md#v2) |
| [3](BBE.md#v3) | By his foolish behaviour a man's ways are turned upside down, and his heart is bitter against the Lord. | [WEB](WEB.md#v3) · [KJV](KJV.md#v3) · [ASV](ASV.md#v3) |
| [4](BBE.md#v4) | Wealth makes a great number of friends; but the poor man is parted from his friend. | [WEB](WEB.md#v4) · [KJV](KJV.md#v4) · [ASV](ASV.md#v4) |
| [5](BBE.md#v5) | A false witness will not go without punishment, and the breather out of deceit will not go free. | [WEB](WEB.md#v5) · [KJV](KJV.md#v5) · [ASV](ASV.md#v5) |
| [6](BBE.md#v6) | Great numbers will make attempts to get the approval of a ruler: and every man is the special friend of him who has something to give. | [WEB](WEB.md#v6) · [KJV](KJV.md#v6) · [ASV](ASV.md#v6) |
| [7](BBE.md#v7) | All the brothers of the poor man are against him: how much more do his friends go far from him! ... | [WEB](WEB.md#v7) · [KJV](KJV.md#v7) · [ASV](ASV.md#v7) |
| [8](BBE.md#v8) | He who gets wisdom has love for his soul: he who keeps good sense will get what is truly good. | [WEB](WEB.md#v8) · [KJV](KJV.md#v8) · [ASV](ASV.md#v8) |
| [9](BBE.md#v9) | A false witness will not go without punishment, and the breather out of deceit will be cut off. | [WEB](WEB.md#v9) · [KJV](KJV.md#v9) · [ASV](ASV.md#v9) |
| [10](BBE.md#v10) | Material comfort is not good for the foolish; much less for a servant to be put over rulers. | [WEB](WEB.md#v10) · [KJV](KJV.md#v10) · [ASV](ASV.md#v10) |
| [11](BBE.md#v11) | A man's good sense makes him slow to wrath, and the overlooking of wrongdoing is his glory. | [WEB](WEB.md#v11) · [KJV](KJV.md#v11) · [ASV](ASV.md#v11) |
| [12](BBE.md#v12) | The king's wrath is like the loud cry of a lion, but his approval is like dew on the grass. | [WEB](WEB.md#v12) · [KJV](KJV.md#v12) · [ASV](ASV.md#v12) |
| [13](BBE.md#v13) | A foolish son is the destruction of his father; and the bitter arguments of a wife are like drops of rain falling without end. | [WEB](WEB.md#v13) · [KJV](KJV.md#v13) · [ASV](ASV.md#v13) |
| [14](BBE.md#v14) | House and wealth are a heritage from fathers, but a wife with good sense is from the Lord. | [WEB](WEB.md#v14) · [KJV](KJV.md#v14) · [ASV](ASV.md#v14) |
| [15](BBE.md#v15) | Hate of work sends deep sleep on a man: and he who has no industry will go without food. | [WEB](WEB.md#v15) · [KJV](KJV.md#v15) · [ASV](ASV.md#v15) |
| [16](BBE.md#v16) | He who keeps the law keeps his soul; but death will be the fate of him who takes no note of the word. | [WEB](WEB.md#v16) · [KJV](KJV.md#v16) · [ASV](ASV.md#v16) |
| [17](BBE.md#v17) | He who has pity on the poor gives to the Lord, and the Lord will give him his reward. | [WEB](WEB.md#v17) · [KJV](KJV.md#v17) · [ASV](ASV.md#v17) |
| [18](BBE.md#v18) | Give your son training while there is hope; let not your heart be purposing his death. | [WEB](WEB.md#v18) · [KJV](KJV.md#v18) · [ASV](ASV.md#v18) |
| [19](BBE.md#v19) | A man of great wrath will have to take his punishment: for if you get him out of trouble you will have to do it again. | [WEB](WEB.md#v19) · [KJV](KJV.md#v19) · [ASV](ASV.md#v19) |
| [20](BBE.md#v20) | Let your ear be open to suggestion and take teaching, so that at the end you may be wise. | [WEB](WEB.md#v20) · [KJV](KJV.md#v20) · [ASV](ASV.md#v20) |
| [21](BBE.md#v21) | A man's heart may be full of designs, but the purpose of the Lord is unchanging. | [WEB](WEB.md#v21) · [KJV](KJV.md#v21) · [ASV](ASV.md#v21) |
| [22](BBE.md#v22) | The ornament of a man is his mercy, and a poor man is better than one who is false. | [WEB](WEB.md#v22) · [KJV](KJV.md#v22) · [ASV](ASV.md#v22) |
| [23](BBE.md#v23) | The fear of the Lord gives life: and he who has it will have need of nothing; no evil will come his way. | [WEB](WEB.md#v23) · [KJV](KJV.md#v23) · [ASV](ASV.md#v23) |
| [24](BBE.md#v24) | The hater of work puts his hand deep into the basin, and will not even take it to his mouth again. | [WEB](WEB.md#v24) · [KJV](KJV.md#v24) · [ASV](ASV.md#v24) |
| [25](BBE.md#v25) | When blows overtake the man of pride, the simple will get sense; say sharp words to the wise, and knowledge will be made clear to him. | [WEB](WEB.md#v25) · [KJV](KJV.md#v25) · [ASV](ASV.md#v25) |
| [26](BBE.md#v26) | He who is violent to his father, driving away his mother, is a son causing shame and a bad name. | [WEB](WEB.md#v26) · [KJV](KJV.md#v26) · [ASV](ASV.md#v26) |
| [27](BBE.md#v27) | A son who no longer gives attention to teaching is turned away from the words of knowledge. | [WEB](WEB.md#v27) · [KJV](KJV.md#v27) · [ASV](ASV.md#v27) |
| [28](BBE.md#v28) | A good-for-nothing witness makes sport of the judge's decision: and the mouth of evil-doers sends out evil like a stream. | [WEB](WEB.md#v28) · [KJV](KJV.md#v28) · [ASV](ASV.md#v28) |
| [29](BBE.md#v29) | Rods are being made ready for the man of pride, and blows for the back of the foolish. | [WEB](WEB.md#v29) · [KJV](KJV.md#v29) · [ASV](ASV.md#v29) |
